summary: Observe cached preview-to-full transitions even when the status text remains unchanged.

decisions.decision: For packaged cached-full evidence, observe the document body and accept completion only when the status is cached and the Preview-quality badge is absent. Probe immediately after the click as well as on DOM mutations. Retain zero added Console runs and the under-100-ms painted-frame requirement. Add a runtime restart regression proving the disk-tier result is returned without invoking the engine.
decisions.rationale: After project reopen, an automatic preview can already be a cache hit. A subsequent full-quality cache hit then leaves the text 'Rendered ... (3d, cached)' unchanged and truthfully removes only the Preview-quality badge. Watching the status node alone times out despite a successful full cache transition. The badge is the product's normative FR-4.3 quality disclosure, while the independent runtime test prevents the broader oracle from masking an engine invocation.

testEvidence.criterion: A cached preview followed by a cached full render must complete when only the Preview-quality badge changes.
testEvidence.test: Execute the real cachedPaint DOM script in happy-dom with a pre-existing cached status, visible Preview-quality badge, and a Full render control that removes only the badge.

red.command: npm.cmd test -- --run tests/scripts/m4-packaged-walkthrough.test.ts -t "observes a cached full render when only the preview-quality badge changes"
red.outcome: RED: 1 failed by the five-second test bound because the oracle observed only the unchanged status node.

green.command: npm.cmd test -- --run tests/scripts/m4-packaged-walkthrough.test.ts -t "observes a cached full render when only the preview-quality badge changes"
green.outcome: GREEN: 1/1 passed in 13 ms after observing the body, quality badge, and immediate post-click state.

testEvidence.criterion: The broader cached-transition oracle must not hide a real cold-start engine invocation.
testEvidence.test: Share disk storage and persisted project consent between two workbench runtimes; render in the first, then request the same preview in the restarted runtime.

red.command: Not applicable: this is an independent characterization guard following the behavioral oracle's recorded RED.
red.outcome: No synthetic product failure was created; the test confirms the cache layer was already correct.

green.outcome: GREEN characterization: 31/31 passed; the restarted runtime returned cached success and made zero engine render calls.

testEvidence.criterion: The combined M4 harness and runtime cache regressions remain green.
testEvidence.test: Run the three M4 harness suites plus the workbench runtime suite once.

red.command: Not applicable: this is the combined verification gate following the recorded behavioral RED/GREEN cycle.
red.outcome: No synthetic aggregate failure was created.

nearMisses.sought: Exact clean packaged native M4 cold-start cache evidence
nearMisses.confirmation: The run passed target-identity thumbnail selection and restart persistence, then timed out only because cached preview and cached full shared the same status text; host cleanup passed.

questions: No owner decision is required; the change binds the oracle to the existing Preview-quality disclosure and retains an independent zero-engine-call proof.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
